title: LeoIoT Dashboard
tagline: Monitoring stack for room climate, PV data, and live building telemetry at HTL Leonding.
highlights: MQTT-first telemetry pipeline for room sensors and generated demo data
InfluxDB + Grafana for time-series storage and visualization
3D building explorer and dashboard-v2 frontend for live room status
Quarkus-based sensor publisher and WebSocket bridge for browser clients
Docker Compose environment that runs the full platform locally

services.name: Mosquitto
services.purpose: MQTT broker for sensor traffic and generated room data.

services.name: InfluxDB
services.purpose: Time-series storage for room climate and energy telemetry.

services.name: Telegraf
services.purpose: Subscription and write pipeline from MQTT into InfluxDB.

services.name: Grafana
services.purpose: Operational dashboards for analytics and history.

services.name: Frontend
services.purpose: Three.js building view for room temperatures.

services.name: Dashboard-v2
services.purpose: Live room dashboard for charts and sensor cards.

services.name: MQTT WebSocket Bridge
services.purpose: Pushes temperature and CO2 updates to browser subscribers.

services.name: Quarkus Sensor Generator
services.purpose: Publishes sine data and room temperatures on a schedule.

services.name: Fake Sensors
services.purpose: Simulates realistic room occupancy, temperature, and CO2 values.

services.name: Solax Collector
services.purpose: Pulls PV plant data from Solax Cloud and writes it to InfluxDB.

api.name: Sensor Snapshot
api.description: Documented test request for sensor data on localhost:8080.

api.name: CO2 Topics
api.description: Plain numeric CO2 measurements per room.

api.name: Browser Live Feed
api.description: Clients subscribe to specific rooms and receive cached snapshots plus updates.

setup: Run `docker compose up -d` from the repository root.
Open the main applications on ports 8080, 8081, 3000, and 8086.
Use `docker compose logs -f dashboard-v2` or `docker compose logs -f fake-sensors` during development.
Start frontend packages independently with `npm install` and `npm run dev` inside `frontend/` or `dashboard-v2/`.
Run the Quarkus generator with `./mvnw quarkus:dev` in `backend/sensor-data-generator/`.
deployment: A self-hosted workflow rebuilds the Docker deployment on pushes to `main`.
GitHub Pages publishes this Astro site at the repository project path.
Nginx on the school VM routes `/`, `/dashboard/`, `/influx/`, and `/ws` to internal services.

presentation.title: Project Handover
presentation.body: The team took over an existing LeoGreen effort and refocused it on an operable school monitoring platform.

presentation.title: Problem Space
presentation.body: The project combines room-climate monitoring for classrooms with PV and energy-related telemetry.

presentation.title: Architecture
presentation.body: Sensors and generators publish to MQTT, Telegraf writes to InfluxDB, and dashboards consume the resulting data.

presentation.title: Sensor Hardware
presentation.body: ESP-based sensor boxes provide room measurements that can be visualized in dashboards and 3D views.

presentation.title: Tech Stack
presentation.body: The solution uses MQTT, InfluxDB, Grafana, Quarkus, Node services, and browser-based frontends.
